payload.character_role: Veterinary Ethologist specializing in feline behavioral ecology and nutritional physiology

story.logline: A Veterinary Ethologist delves into the evolutionary biology and domestication of cats, revealing how these factors dictate their unique nutritional needs and guide the selection of optimal cat foods like Purina and Pro Plan.
clip_beats: Introduce the evolutionary lineage and domestication process of Felis catus, emphasizing how ancestral hunting behaviors inform current dietary requirements.
Examine feline anatomical adaptations such as dentition and digestive tract morphology, linking them to specific nutrient needs and the formulation of premium cat foods.

prompt_additions: Include detailed visual focus on feline dental structure and musculature during feeding to illustrate anatomical adaptations.
Highlight behavioral sequences demonstrating natural hunting instincts and their influence on feeding behavior.
Showcase natural habitats alongside domestic settings to contrast wild dietary sources with commercial food options.
Incorporate subtle observational cues of social interaction during feeding to reflect social structure influences.
Capture close-up shots of food textures and ingredients to correlate with nutritional biochemistry concepts.
